_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Participate in control system incident and disaster response, including secure system recovery.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including policy development, role and responsibility definition, escalation protocols, exception management, and board-level model risk reporting.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.468), DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.437).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.7/10 (raw 0.267). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [control, disaster, incident, participate, recovery, response] vs WIDAI-only terms [board, definition, designing, development, escalation, exception] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.883, R=0.856). P exceeds R by 0.027 — the DCWF element's 12 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 26 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.7%). "Participate in control system incident and disaster response, including secure s" and "Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.473 > STS 0.267 (gap +0.206). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [control, disaster, incident, participate, recovery, response]. WIDAI terms: [board, definition, designing, development, escalation, exception].
